Key_Result: Grow active provider base from 10K to 15K by end of Q1
Update_Notes: Strong momentum in TX and FL markets. Added 840 providers in Feb. Recruitment campaigns performing 12% above target CPA. Pipeline looks healthy for March close.

Objective: Expand Provider Network to 15K Active Therapists
Key_Result: Increase provider retention rate from 82% to 90%
Update_Notes: Retention dipped in Jan due to reimbursement delays with Aetna. Working with insurance ops to resolve. Need to monitor closely.

Objective: Expand Provider Network to 15K Active Therapists
Key_Result: Launch in 3 new states (CO; NV; OR) by end of Q1
Update_Notes: CO and NV fully live. OR credentialing in progress -- 78% of initial provider cohort approved. On track for mid-March launch.

Objective: Expand Provider Network to 15K Active Therapists
Key_Result: Reduce average provider onboarding time from 21 days to 14 days
Increase monthly active patients from 85K to 110K
Update_Notes: Paid channels driving strong acquisition. Organic up 18% MoM from SEO investments. Feb was best month ever for new patient signups (14.2K).

Key_Result: Improve patient 90-day retention from 60% to 72%
Update_Notes: Retention improved 4pts since Q4 but pace is slowing. Biggest drop-off still at sessions 3-4. Testing new re-engagement nudges and therapist matching improvements.

Key_Result: Achieve patient NPS of 72+
Update_Notes: NPS trending up. Major driver is reduced wait times for first appointment (down to 3.2 days avg). Detractors mostly cite billing confusion.

Key_Result: Launch self-serve appointment rescheduling for 100% of patients
Update_Notes: Rolled out to 80% of patient base. Remaining 20% on legacy booking system -- migration scheduled for early March. No major bugs in rollout so far.

Key_Result: Add 4 new national insurance partners (total pipeline: Cigna; Humana; Kaiser; Molina)
Update_Notes: Cigna contract signed and integration in flight. Humana LOI expected mid-March. Kaiser conversations stalled -- need exec escalation. Molina deprioritized due to low volume potential. Exploring Centene as replacement.

Key_Result: Increase gross revenue to $28M quarterly run-rate
Update_Notes: $24.5M through Feb annualized. Gap driven by slower-than-expected Cigna ramp and lower session volume in Jan (holiday effect). March pipeline looks strong but we need a big month to close the gap.

Key_Result: Reduce claim denial rate from 8% to 4%
Update_Notes: Down from 8% to 5.8%. New pre-submission validation catching 60% of common errors. Biggest remaining issue is incorrect CPT code selection by providers -- training module launching March 3.

Key_Result: Decrease average claim processing time from 35 days to 21 days
Update_Notes: Reduced to 28 days avg. Automation of ERA posting saving ~4 days. UHC and BCBS now under 21 days. Aetna still lagging at 38 days due to manual review requirements on their end.

Objective: Improve Platform Reliability and Developer Velocity
Key_Result: Achieve 99.95% uptime for patient-facing services
Update_Notes: Two incidents in Feb: 45-min booking outage on 2/12 (DB failover issue) and 20-min payment processing delay on 2/22 (third-party gateway). 99.91% MTD. Implementing circuit breakers and additional redundancy.

Update_Notes: Design approved. Data model 70% complete. Blocked on finalizing metric definitions with finance and ops. Targeting mid-March for v1 but at risk of slipping to April.

Update_Notes: 7 of 10 events instrumented and flowing. Remaining: insurance verification callback; therapist match acceptance; and post-session survey completion. ETA end of Feb.

Update_Notes: Jan and Feb reports delivered on time. Added new segmentation by insurance type and referral source. March report will include first cut of therapist-matching quality correlation.

Update_Notes: Migrated 3 of 8 core pipelines to streaming. Remaining pipelines have complex dependencies on batch insurance data feeds. Need to negotiate real-time API access with payer partners.
